Four Corners/Ambrosia/Pajariot 500 kV transmission line : environmental impact statement.

The Four Corners-Ambrosia-Pajarito 500 kV Transmission Project proposes to build a 173-mile transmission line in northwestern New Mexico. The line will connect the Four Corners Power Plant to a new station near Albuquerque, aiming to enhance electricity transmission capacity in the region. While the project promises economic benefits and improved power supply, it raises concerns about environmental impacts on soil, vegetation, and cultural resources. Measures are suggested to mitigate these effects, but the project has sparked debate over its cumulative impact on the region's environment and communities.
Northwestern New Mexico, from Four Corners Power Plant to Albuquerque
Public Service Company of New Mexico

The Four Corners-Ambrosia-Pajarito 500 kV Transmission Project was proposed to improve electricity transmission capacity in northwestern New Mexico, connecting the Four Corners Power Plant to Albuquerque. While it aimed to bring economic benefits and enhance power supply, it faced significant public concern regarding its environmental impacts, particularly on local soil, vegetation, and cultural resources. The debate surrounding the project highlighted the tension between energy infrastructure development and environmental preservation.
United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs

"Citizens who battled the construction of this first 765-kilovolt line in New York have long contended that the Commission's original decision did not go far enough, and have adamantly insisted that there should be a moratorium on such lines until the research program has been completed." — Public Comments – Section 5.2
